entries.evidence_summary: Authority is expressed as a strict operative precedence chain, with reference evidence separated under its own heading.
Material pushback is defined once in guardrails and reused for freeze and terminal-state semantics.
Outputs are split into durable canonical artifacts, invocation-bound runtime promotion targets, and run-scoped evidence with distinct lifecycles.
Each terminal state has an explicit trigger predicate tied to run evidence and separated from the others.
The self-check is limited to executed structural and lexical heuristic checks, records keywords and evidence, and explicitly does not claim semantic validation.
Scope exclusions remain deduplicated, Step 1 requires normalization and deduplication, and guardrails require alignment with the paired contract.
Arbitration is defined as minor-only, leader-mediated, evidence-bearing, unable to override reject, and able to produce only frozen_with_conditions.
All canonical package artifacts are listed with full tool-relative paths under tools/agent-role-builder/role/.
Completion criteria are defined separately for frozen, frozen_with_conditions, pushback, blocked, and resume_required.
Context gathering is labeled as preconditions before Step 1, and numbered execution starts at validation.
Carried-forward items are tracked by stable finding ID in resume handling, fix-items maps, and terminal closeout.
Update and fix operations append dated decision-log entries and replace the board summary only on promotion, preserving prior history.
Every numbered step is explicitly labeled as target-state behavior and guarded against unexecuted pass claims.
Learning and diff artifacts are required even on rejecting or budget-exhaustion closeout rounds, avoiding revision-only gating.
The outputs section is a single artifact matrix that declares artifact class, canonical path or root, and write lifecycle by operation and terminal state.
Local authority order is derived from controller, runtime contract, component governance bundle, and shared review contract, while shared review semantics are inherited by reference.
The role identity states the enduring mission of the Agent Role Builder rather than current run state or recovery context.
Writable scope is anchored to the runtime contract and names the durable canonical root, run-evidence root, and explicit runtime targets only.
Authority layers use operative governance sources only, while reference documents and runtime knobs are classified separately.
Governance mechanics are limited to inherited contracts and source-backed local consequences, including invoker-controlled conditional promotion.
Scope uses separate inclusion and exclusion lists, and each out-of-scope bullet is exclusion-only.
The governed review round is defined as an ordered sub-sequence with explicit per-round artifact writes for compliance, fix items, review, learning, diff, and postmortem evidence.
The role declares the local governance bundle it must load and identifies shared review obligations as inherited rather than restated in full.
Required inputs enumerate reviewer roster, review mode, budget controls, writable roots, targets, governance bundle, shared contract, governance config, and runtime config.
